notes: Inferred from 2025 betting-plays section: Publisher to Show $$$$$, Sovereignty to Show $$, Final Gambit to Win $.
analysis:
There are a lot of horses that are not only speed horses, but ones that have to have the lead in order to run their best race. If they push the pace early as expected, it should open up the door for the closers to make a run at the end.

This is the first Derby I truly don't see any horses that look unbeatable. Whichever of the closing horses is able to sit down closest off the front group will have the advantage. Journalism and Sovereignty historically sit in that point in the field.

Value is in taking Publisher to hit the board and finish top 3 — should pay close to 5-1. Sovereignty to Show would pay about 5-2. At least one of these will cash. Then for fun I'll sprinkle a longshot in Final Gambit to win at about 20-1.
betting_plays:

Exacta
Journalism / Publisher, Sovereignty, Final Gambit (3 ways)

Trifecta

Superfecta
Journalism / Publisher / Final Gambit, Baeza, Rodriguez / Final Gambit, Baeza, Rodriguez, Sovereignty, Sandman, East Avenue, Neoequos, Luxor Café, Tiztastic (24 ways)

horses.name: (none)
horses.note: nobody is great this year — wide open which is why I'd rather sprinkle longshots

horses.name: Sovereignty
horses.note: most consistent horse, could win with a good trip

horses.name: Sandman
horses.note: looked great in last race, but I trust Sovereignty more

horses.name: Journalism
horses.note: no blemishes on resume and will be bet heavy

power_rankings.tier: Tier 3 — Closers that should take advantage of the fast pace breaking down

horses.name: Publisher
horses.note: my underdog. Will 100% outrun his odds

horses.name: Baeza
horses.note: improving and exciting horse who could outrun everyone

horses.name: Tiztastic
horses.note: improved and will handle the distance well

horses.name: Final Gambit
horses.note: wild card. Amazing closing speed

horses.name: Grande
horses.note: lightly raced and keeps improving — capable

horses.name: Burnham Square
horses.note: capable, but of the closers there are better

horses.name: Luxor Café
horses.note: might be the real deal but passing on Japan

horses.name: Chunk of Gold
horses.note: did well in Louisiana, but better proven horses ahead

horses.name: Rodriguez
horses.note: took step up in the Wood, tougher trip this go

horses.name: American Promise
horses.note: looked great against bad competition. Don't expect much

horses.name: East Avenue
horses.note: likeliest of the speed horses if I had to back one

horses.name: Neoequos
horses.note: expect him near the top all the way til last turn

horses.name: Citizen Bull
horses.note: regressed each race. Distance is an issue

horses.name: Coal Battle
horses.note: can't do the distance

horses.name: Owen Almighty
horses.note: will sprint out and float to last

horses.name: Flying Mohawk
horses.note: nonfactor

horses.name: Render Judgment
horses.note: bad horse

horses.name: Admire Daytona
horses.note: Japan #2 horse, pass

horses.final_take: He’s a regressing horse in a tough post position who also will have trouble with the distance. Pass
horses.writeup:
Bob Baffert Horse. First time he is back allowed to the derby which will be a storyline. This is one of his two horses
Had a much better 2024 campaign but hasn’t put it together in 2025 yet
Was on a three-race graded stakes win streak prior to losing Santa Anita
Looked great in the wins setting the pace and winning a couple of those wire-to-wire
* Speed figures are overstated out of the California circuit. Take the high brisnet with a grain of salt
Tough draw getting the first post position. Will have to shoot for the front and get a rail run
The distance will be the issue here. Has looked great in 1M and 1 1/16 M races but the extra furlong in the santa anita killed him. Adding a full extra ¼ on top of that at Churchill seems unlikely to help

horses.final_take: I like how he positions himself, I could see him be leading around the final turn prior to fading. May use underneath to help juice up some tickets
horses.writeup:
Flew to the front in the Florida Derby and each other race. He actually kept his pace well throughout but got beat by a better horse in Tiztastic by a little
He will try and jump out to the front along with the other speed horses
The expectation is that he won’t be able to keep his pace up throughout the race into the back stretch
I do like how he positions himself as a stalker which is where I think will yield the best positioning this year
He breaks well which is important for the horses trying to get into the lead early
Hasn’t proven he has the distance which is why he is going off as a long shot

horses.final_take: Wild card here. I do like this horse so I will sprinkle some on him to win, but mostly just include in exotics
horses.writeup:
Started racing on grass and most recently won the Jeff Ruby on synthetic dirt. This will be his first race on actual dirt which is the main question mark
He went from last to an easy first in the Jeff Ruby in an impressive final stretch
Will be a wild card
Rookie jockey is concerning
Posted the fasted MPH (37) over the final stretch of any prep race
The extra distance will only benefit him as his best speed is at the end
If he can translate to dirt with ease he may have a live chance

horses.final_take: Don’t buy the Bob Baffert Kool-Aid but may include him far underneath in some superfectas
horses.writeup:
Bob Baffert Horse
Hasn’t looked impressive at all until leaving the west coast circuit and winning at the Wood
Got dogged by Journalism in prior race, and beat easy by Citizen Bull in race before that
His prior races looking meh might discount everything from the Wood memorial, including Grande who he beat
I tried to like this horse, but can’t get to the point where I would place anything on him
Drew post 3 and will be forced to gun for the lead from the start. He either breaks well and gets out front and will use up too much energy and falter, or breaks poorly and never recover
Maybe because I hate Bob Baffert that I’m staying away from him but I don’t think he’s the best horse

horses.last_race: Won Virginia Derby? Idk what this is
horses.final_take: simple stay away from this horse. Only races he did well in were against bad fields
horses.writeup:
Looked fast in this, but had no traffic issues at all and just ran away from the field, which was not an impressive stakes race. Highly doubt it’ll be as easy in the Derby
Any time a horse comes in with 7+ races it feels like he’s not an absolute dawg and the owner is just desperate for derby qualifying points and prize money
Coming into the Derby with over 6 weeks of rest also historically hinders momentum from prior racing

horses.last_race: Won Fukuryu Stakes convincingly by 10 lengths and extending lead. Not sure about the quality of competition but this horse looked strong and in control
horses.final_take: Not going to bet a Japanese Horse and will make him beat me
horses.writeup:
Has lost to other Japanese Horse Luxor Café twice
This horse is not going to get any looks so will probably go off closer to 50-1. I also will not waste time on him, don’t waste any of your money on him either.
Luxor Café (15-1)
Also beat the other Japan horse, Admire Daytona.
I actually am tempted to include him in some of my plays. The biggest concern I have is that he is not receiving a ton of hype like the top Japanese horse has garnered each of the past couple of years. People usually are dying to say that Japan will eventually win one and prop up expectations for their top contender, although I haven’t heard much Luxor Café chatter which might be telling
Passes every eye test from his past races with closing speed

horses.last_race: Won Santa Anita Derby, half length over Baeza
horses.final_take: Will go off as heavy favorite because of no blemishes on his resume. Will use him on top of some exacta and trifectas
horses.writeup:
Will be betting favorite at Post time
Looked impressive winning on the west coast circuit
There is a lot to like when watching this horse. He stays just off the pace and always looks in control. Comes from behind and surges past the leader every time
My concerns stack up (grading him toughly since he’s the betting favorite)
Inexperienced Trainer (0-1 at Derby)
Inexperienced Jockey (only two starts with 9th as best finish)
Speed figures are over-inflated since on California speed tracks (not saying hes not fast. Just saying he’s not that fast)
Hasn’t raced against a large field like many of the east-coast prep races get experience from. Hasn’t faced true traffic
He very well could be the best horse and win. But I wouldn’t bet him outright to win

horses.last_race: Won Blue Grass Stakes by a nose
horses.final_take: Good not great horse, expecting him to finish 5-7 range
horses.writeup:
Experienced jockey in Brian Hernandez Jr
Most Derby Leaderboard qualifying points
Just means he placed in the most prep races, but still is noteworthy
Breaks well, but always checks quick to the back. It’s encouraging that he doesn’t start in the back because solely because of bad breaks. In the Derby I could see him break well and check to the exact spot he wants to be to run his race.
I don’t know if he has the quality to complete the comebacks against a larger Derby field, but he certainly will run a good race and will finish top 10
Compared to the other closers in the field, he doesn’t look like he was a takeover closer who – if there was the extra 1/8th mile – would have continued to blow past the field. I usually want my closers taking over and extending leads

horses.final_take: Lightly raced makes him a wild card. Could hit the board for sure. Maybe will sprinkle him into exotics
horses.writeup:
Won two allowance races before stepping up in class in the Wood Memorial
Lightly raced and still has room for improvement after doing so each time
Had a much tougher trip than Rodriguez in the Wood but still looked just as strong in the last stretch
If you like Rodriguez you should also be betting Grande
He will be a popular mid-tier long shot pick

horses.last_race: 2nd Jeff Ruby Stakes to Final Gambit. Got beat by 4 lengths in last stretch
horses.writeup:
Jason Worth’s horse
Not much to look into here – won’t hit the board
Also a synthetic dirt only horse
Rookie Jockey

horses.last_race: 2nd Blue Grass to Burnham Square by a nose after leading the entire race
horses.final_take: He drew a good post position to break and dive towards the front if he needs to, but the two races he got killed in feel more likely to repeat themselves than him holding up in the front. Will still use underneath
horses.writeup:
Held pace entire Blue Grass until getting caught at very end
Earned a lot of early hype at the end of the 2024 racing year by rolling early in the Breeder’s Futurity
Issue for East Avenue is that his best three races in his career he was in first from the very beginning and did not have to chase anyone down. In one of the two races he broke horribly and could not find any momentum to get back in the race. This was not inspiring, but isn’t the end of the world, bad breaks happen
The issue is his second to last race where he actually broke well but sat down early in 2nd place and still couldn’t figure out how to chase down a 40-1 horse (albeit that horse ended up winning by a lot). Not only could he not chase down the pace setting horse, but he seemed like he gave up near the end and has no winning mentality

horses.final_take: Absolutely will bet on him. Betting him to show may pay out about 3 or 4 to 1 and feels my favorite play in the derby. Will sprinkle him to win as well. Will also look for some good H2H prop bets for him against a speed horse like Coal Battle or Citizen Bull. Must use in exotics as well
horses.writeup:
2nd straight race racing just behind Sandman
Honestly didn’t look much worse than Sandman. Just broke around the turn after him and then kept pace as they both ran past the rest of the field
Another interesting note is he has never won a race and broke his maiden… one such horse hasn’t won since 1938ish.
Nonetheless, you might be able to get a really good price on this horse who is only improving
Would suggest taking him in Head-to-Head matchups if can find that somewhere and also using underneath
Each race I watch of him I easily find something to like and a reason he could outrun his past performances. Got squeezed in the rebel stakes or else would have been tied with Sandman.
In the race prior (southwest stakes) he was making the best rail run of anyone and got blatantly cut off which stuck him in 6th. Otherwise may have won. Could have catapulted him into being a top 5 horse favorite
Son of American Pharoah
Good trainer in Steve Asmussan and Vet jockey in Irad Ortiz

horses.final_take: I want to like him but he is just another good not great horse. Going to pass
horses.writeup:
Has a good amount of experience racing against strong fields
Winning the Louisiana is always a strong indicator because it is the longest of the Derby prep races (1 3/16 mile) prior the Derby (1 ¼). Tiztastic came from behind against an early fast pace which played into his favor
In other prep races, he has raced strongly side by side to Sandman
Has dealt with large fields and a good amount of traffic well

Also got beat easy by American Promise in a weak field
Only is in the derby due to three horses scratching

horses.last_race: 3rd Arkansas Derby to Sandman. Race got out to a blazing pace but he ran his race how he wanted and made his move to take the lead at final turn, but got outrun by Sandman and Publisher at the last stretch. I don’t see why he would all of a sudden flip that on them in the Derby
horses.final_take: Does not have the distance in him, pass
horses.writeup:
Doesn’t have the distance in him. I do think he is a strong horse and could win if this race was a furlong shorter, but the last 1/8th is going to keep him off my board
Has won a lot of races in his career, but all shorter in length
Rookie jockey on him is the icing on the cake to not take him

horses.last_race: Won Arkansas Derby running down the pace setters who set out a blistering pace. Made for a perfect scenario for his closing style
horses.final_take: Very likeable horse, will only include in exotics underneath. Runs too green and loses momentum easy down final stretches when it counts most.
horses.writeup:
Jockey: Jose Ortiz
Going to go off as one of the favorites. Has looked like the strongest closer in every race recently
Runs a little green though and has a history of bad breaks
If he can make his move to push the lead at the last turn he could win
Drew the forbidden post 17 which miraculously has never generated a winner
I actually like the outside post position for him since he usually makes his move around the outside lanes
If he can sit down in the middle of the pack after the first turn I think he could absolutely win

horses.final_take: This horse does not run bad races – will also use up top on exotics
horses.writeup:
Tappan Street moved first and Sovereignty ran out of room in the chase. If track had the extra 1/8th like the Derby will, I think Sovereignty would have passed him as the better horse
Didn’t break his maiden until jumping up in class in a graded stakes and hit the board in each of the three graded races
Has been hot in his last couple races this year. Lost the Florida Derby slightly to a horse who already scratched from the Kentucky Derby. Sounds familiar to Mage winning the Derby after Forte scratched…
Has a big win at the end of 2024 at Churchill Downs with an extending lead down the stretch over Sandman
Beat Burnham Square in Fountain of Youth with impressive last furlong
He feels like a safe bet to hit the board
I like how he positions himself. If can get to mid pack at the first turn I like him

horses.final_take: Not going to win unless has a major step forward. Pass
horses.writeup:
Lightly raced, has come in 2nd in past three races after breaking his maiden
I don’t think he will flop. Could see him outrunning his odds and finishing top 10

horses.last_race: Neck behind Journalism in Santa Anita
horses.final_take: Use underneath..  also has an outside chance to win. If goes off at 10-1 or higher I will take to win
horses.writeup:
Pretty heavily raced horse including three YTD already which I don’t love. Doesn’t seem to have the stamina to make the full 1 ¼ Mile race at Churchill
Will try and press early and fade at the end hard
Only is in this race because the Owner is desperate to have a Derby horse. He should be entered in a shorter race.
Baeza (8-1) ** Also Eligible – Is next into the field if a horse scratches
I usually don’t include Also Eligible horses in my writeups but this one legit could win if gets into the field. Only didn’t qualify because his 2nd place finish at Santa Anita was docked Derby Prep points because it was a smaller field
If you like Journalism you should like this horse too. Ran exact same race as Journalism in last race but just lost
Lightly raced so not much to go off of
Inflated speed figures from California
Has only run 1M races until 1 1/8 santa anita, although distance didn’t seem like an issue
This thing cost like 1.5 Million. Was made and bought for this race.
Runs green – but is still maturing. Either shows up and places or could fall in pack in 10th
